question: A network user submits input that should trigger an IMS business program. Which actions align with Redbook IMS principles? [Hard-1]
option_1: Require synchronous response to every IMS input message
option_2: Assume only terminal users on same z/OS can access IMS TM applications
option_3: Use system services for dispatching/loading programs and locking support
option_4: Use APPC interface handling to bridge synchronous expectation with IMS asynchronous protocol

question: IMS cannot process incoming data immediately during peak load. Which actions align with Redbook IMS principles? [Hard-2]
option_1: Require synchronous response to every IMS input message
option_2: Use system services for dispatching/loading programs and locking support
option_3: Use APPC interface handling to bridge synchronous expectation with IMS asynchronous protocol
option_4: Disable dependent address spaces and rely on a single control thread

question: An APPC-integrated flow expects immediate replies but IMS is asynchronous. Which actions align with Redbook IMS principles? [Hard-3]
option_1: Queue delayed input/output messages external to IMS until processing/delivery confirmation
option_2: Use APPC interface handling to bridge synchronous expectation with IMS asynchronous protocol
option_3: Treat input as a transaction message that can trigger IMS application processing
option_4: Assume only terminal users on same z/OS can access IMS TM applications

question: An enterprise runs IMS across multiple platforms and address spaces. Which actions align with Redbook IMS principles? [Hard-4]
option_1: Use APPC interface handling to bridge synchronous expectation with IMS asynchronous protocol
option_2: Treat input as a transaction message that can trigger IMS application processing
option_3: Use system services for dispatching/loading programs and locking support
option_4: Assume only terminal users on same z/OS can access IMS TM applications

question: Operations wants resilient transaction handling during subsystem failures. Which actions align with Redbook IMS principles? [Hard-5]
option_1: Assume only terminal users on same z/OS can access IMS TM applications
option_2: Queue delayed input/output messages external to IMS until processing/delivery confirmation
option_3: Require synchronous response to every IMS input message
option_4: Use system services for dispatching/loading programs and locking support

option_1: Treat input as a transaction message that can trigger IMS application processing
option_2: Use IMS system services for restart/recovery and security controls
option_3: Allow asynchronous processing; immediate reply is not always required
option_4: Require synchronous response to every IMS input message

option_2: Delete unprocessed messages immediately to reduce queue depth
option_3: Use IMS system services for restart/recovery and security controls
option_4: Queue delayed input/output messages external to IMS until processing/delivery confirmation

question: An enterprise runs IMS across multiple platforms and address spaces. Which actions align with Redbook IMS principles? [Hard-79]
option_1: Use system services for dispatching/loading programs and locking support
option_2: Assume only terminal users on same z/OS can access IMS TM applications
option_3: Allow asynchronous processing; immediate reply is not always required
option_4: Disable dependent address spaces and rely on a single control thread

question: Operations wants resilient transaction handling during subsystem failures. Which actions align with Redbook IMS principles? [Hard-80]
option_1: Queue delayed input/output messages external to IMS until processing/delivery confirmation
option_2: Treat input as a transaction message that can trigger IMS application processing
option_3: Delete unprocessed messages immediately to reduce queue depth
option_4: Require synchronous response to every IMS input message

question: A network user submits input that should trigger an IMS business program. Which actions align with Redbook IMS principles? [Hard-81]
option_1: Allow asynchronous processing; immediate reply is not always required
option_2: Use IMS system services for restart/recovery and security controls
option_3: Queue delayed input/output messages external to IMS until processing/delivery confirmation
option_4: Use system services for dispatching/loading programs and locking support

question: IMS cannot process incoming data immediately during peak load. Which actions align with Redbook IMS principles? [Hard-82]
option_1: Treat input as a transaction message that can trigger IMS application processing
option_2: Allow asynchronous processing; immediate reply is not always required
option_3: Require synchronous response to every IMS input message
option_4: Use APPC interface handling to bridge synchronous expectation with IMS asynchronous protocol
